atoms.content: Train/Test split divides your dataset into two pieces: a large chunk to train the model, and a smaller, unseen chunk to test its accuracy.
If you give a student a practice test (train data) and then give them the exact same test for the final (test data), you don't know if they learned math or just memorized the answers.
Data leakage occurs when information from the test set accidentally leaks into the training set, resulting in overly optimistic, false performance metrics.

1. Prevents testing on training data.
2. Usually an 80/20 split.
3. Essential for evaluating real-world performance.
Why is testing a model on the exact same data it was trained on a bad idea? (Answer: It encourages memorization instead of true learning).
